articles.headline: Government Unveils New Industrial Policy 2024 to Boost Manufacturing and Global Competitiveness
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the new Industrial Policy 2024, focusing on enhancing India's manufacturing capabilities and integrating into global value chains.
• Key pillars include technology adoption, skill development, ease of doing business, and promotion of green industries.
• The policy aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, targeting a 25% share of manufacturing in GDP by 2030.
• It introduces sector-specific incentives under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme and streamlines regulatory approvals.
• Special emphasis is placed on MSME growth, innovation ecosystems, and sustainable industrial practices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Industrial policy changes and their effects on industrial growth.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: New Industrial Policy 2024 focuses on manufacturing share in GDP (target 25% by 2030).
Key pillars: technology, skills, ease of business, green industries.
Introduces enhanced sector-specific PLI incentives.
Aims to attract both domestic and foreign investment.
Emphasizes MSME growth and innovation ecosystems.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the potential impact of the New Industrial Policy 2024 on India's economic growth and employment generation.
Discuss how the policy addresses challenges of global competitiveness and integration into global value chains.
Examine the role of PLI schemes and skill development initiatives in achieving the policy's objectives.
Assess the policy's focus on green industries and sustainable manufacturing practices in the context of climate change commitments.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a modern factory with 'Make in India' logo and graphs showing economic growth.
articles.trivia: India's first Industrial Policy Resolution was adopted in 1948, followed by a more comprehensive one in 1956, shaping the mixed economy model.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Petitions Seeking Clarification on Electoral Bonds Verdict
articles.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has been constituted to hear petitions seeking clarification and implementation guidelines following its earlier verdict on electoral bonds.
• The petitioners are seeking clarity on the disclosure of donor identities and the mechanism for ensuring transparency in political funding.
• The original verdict in February 2024 had struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme as unconstitutional, citing violation of the right to information.
• This review is crucial for defining the future of political funding transparency in India and ensuring compliance by political parties and the Election Commission.
• The court's observations are expected to provide a roadmap for legislative reforms in campaign finance.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court to review petitions on Electoral Bonds verdict.
Original verdict (Feb 2024) struck down Electoral Bonds Scheme.
Key issues: donor identity disclosure, transparency in political funding.
Involves the Right to Information (Article 19(1)(a)).
Election Commission of India plays a role in implementing electoral reforms.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's original verdict on electoral bonds for democratic transparency and accountability.
Discuss the challenges in ensuring complete transparency in political funding in India and suggest potential reforms.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the anonymity of political donations in a democratic setup.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to electoral processes.
articles.imageDescription: Supreme Court of India building with a gavel in the foreground.
articles.trivia: The first general elections in independent India were held from October 1951 to February 1952.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Hold Joint Economic Commission Meeting, Focus on Trade Expansion and Investment
articles.explainer:
• The 9th India-Brazil Joint Economic Commission (JEC) meeting concluded, emphasizing strengthening bilateral trade and investment ties.
• Discussions focused on diversifying trade basket, reducing non-tariff barriers, and exploring cooperation in agriculture, energy, and defense sectors.
• Both nations reiterated commitment to multilateralism and cooperation within BRICS, G20, and IBSA forums.
• Agreements were signed to promote greater ease of doing business and enhance collaboration in pharmaceuticals and digital technologies.
• The meeting aimed to leverage the strategic partnership to achieve a target of $15 billion in bilateral trade by 2026.

Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

Discuss the opportunities and challenges in expanding bilateral trade and investment between India and Brazil.

Examine how diversifying the trade basket can enhance economic resilience for both nations.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Brazil displayed side-by-side with a handshake overlay.
articles.trivia: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the fifth largest in the world by both area and population.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful landing experiment of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This test validated critical technologies for autonomous landing, precise navigation, and thermal protection systems crucial for future reusable spacecraft.
• RLV technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable.
• The demonstrator, named 'Pushpak,' performed a precise landing at a designated airstrip, simulating return from space.
• This development places India among a select few nations pursuing advanced reusable rocket technology, bolstering its position in the global space market.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.

mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for global space access.
Analyze how RLV technology can contribute to economic development by reducing launch costs and fostering innovation in space applications.
Examine the challenges and opportunities for ISRO in developing and commercializing advanced space technologies like RLV.
Compare India's RLV efforts with those of other major spacefaring nations and assess its future trajectory.
articles.imageDescription: Artist's impression of ISRO's RLV-TD 'Pushpak' landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: ISRO's first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched on 19 April 1975 from Kapustin Yar, a Soviet rocket launch and development site.

articles.headline: Government Releases Updated National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) with Enhanced Targets
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released an updated National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), aligning with India's enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The revised plan introduces more ambitious targets for renewable energy deployment, energy efficiency, and afforestation efforts.
• It integrates climate resilience strategies across various sectors, including agriculture, water resources, and urban development.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology and innovation, promoting green financing, and strengthening international cooperation for climate action.
• The update reflects India's commitment to achieving its climate goals while ensuring sustainable development and inclusive growth.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.

articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing renewable energy sources and a green tree against a backdrop of a city skyline.
articles.trivia: The original NAPCC was launched in 2008, outlining eight national missions to address climate change.

articles.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to remain vigilant on inflation, despite a recent moderation, and reaffirmed commitment to achieving the 4% inflation target.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a focus on reducing liquidity in the banking system.
• Experts suggest the pause aims to support economic growth while ensuring price stability, with future rate cuts dependent on sustained disinflation.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Monetary policy.
prelimsPoints: RBI MPC kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.

mainsPoints: Analyze the rationale behind the RBI MPC's decision to hold the repo rate steady and its implications for India's economic growth and inflation trajectory.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing price stability with supporting economic growth in the current global economic climate.

Examine the role of the Monetary Policy Committee in India's economic governance structure.
articles.imageDescription: Reserve Bank of India logo with an upward trending graph in the background.
articles.trivia: The Reserve Bank of India was established on April 1, 1935, under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.

articles.headline: Government Reviews National Logistics Policy Implementation, Focus on Reducing Costs and Improving Efficiency
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry conducted a comprehensive review of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) implementation, two years after its launch.
• The review highlighted progress in creating unified logistics interface platforms (ULIP) and improving multimodal connectivity.
• Discussions focused on addressing bottlenecks, leveraging technology for better supply chain management, and reducing logistics costs from 14% to 8% of GDP.
• Initiatives like the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan are crucial for achieving the NLP's objectives of integrated infrastructure development.
• The government aims to make India a global logistics hub by 2030, enhancing export competitiveness and domestic trade efficiency.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

articles.imageDescription: Containers being moved by cranes at a busy port, symbolizing logistics.
articles.trivia: India ranks 38th in the Logistics Performance Index (LPI) 2023, a significant improvement from previous years.

articles.headline: Indigenous Dengue Vaccine Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Clinical Trials
articles.explainer:
• An indigenously developed dengue vaccine candidate has completed Phase 3 clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• The vaccine, developed by a leading Indian pharmaceutical company in collaboration with ICMR, targets all four serotypes of the dengue virus.
• This breakthrough is significant for India, which bears a substantial burden of dengue cases annually, and could lead to widespread public health benefits.
• Regulatory approval is expected in the coming months, paving the way for its introduction into the national immunization program.
• The development underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and manufacturing, strengthening its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative.

Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.

articles.trivia: Dengue fever is caused by a virus transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes, which also transmit Zika, chikungunya, and yellow fever viruses.

articles.headline: India and Japan Hold Annual Defence Dialogue, Deepen Strategic Partnership in Indo-Pacific
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan conducted their annual Defence Dialogue, focusing on enhancing bilateral security cooperation and regional stability in the Indo-Pacific.
• Discussions covered joint exercises, defense equipment and technology cooperation, and maritime domain awareness.
• Both nations reaffirmed commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, emphasizing adherence to international law.
• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, particularly in countering common security challenges.
• Future collaborations include joint research in emerging defense technologies and expanded information sharing mechanisms.

articles.headline: Government Finalizes National Cyber Security Strategy 2024 to Safeguard Digital Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has finalized the National Cyber Security Strategy 2024, aiming to create a resilient and secure cyberspace for India.
• The strategy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving government, private sector, academia, and civil society in cyber defense.
• Key focus areas include critical information infrastructure protection, capacity building, R&D in cybersecurity, and international cooperation.
• It proposes a framework for proactive threat intelligence sharing, incident response, and robust regulatory mechanisms.
• The strategy is crucial for protecting India's growing digital economy and ensuring national security in the face of evolving cyber threats.

articles.headline: India Reaffirms Commitment to UN Peacekeeping, Calls for Reforms and Greater Inclusivity
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its unwavering commitment to UN peacekeeping operations during a high-level virtual summit, highlighting its historical contributions as a top troop-contributing country.
• The Indian representative called for comprehensive reforms in UN peacekeeping, including mandate clarity, adequate resources, and enhanced safety for peacekeepers.
• Emphasis was placed on greater inclusivity, particularly the participation of women peacekeepers, to address complex challenges effectively.
• India stressed the importance of technology-driven solutions and capacity building for host countries to ensure sustainable peace.
• This stance aligns with India's broader foreign policy objectives of promoting multilateralism and global peace and security.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.

articles.headline: PM Jan Dhan Yojana Achieves New Milestone, Over 50 Crore Bank Accounts Opened
articles.explainer:
• The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY), India's flagship financial inclusion program, announced the opening of over 50 crore (500 million) bank accounts.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in bringing unbanked populations into the formal financial system, particularly in rural and remote areas.
• The scheme provides access to basic savings accounts, debit cards, direct benefit transfer (DBT) facilities, and micro-insurance/pension products.
• Women account holders constitute a majority of the new accounts, highlighting the scheme's impact on gender financial empowerment.
• PMJDY has been instrumental in enabling direct transfer of government welfare benefits, reducing leakages and improving efficiency.

articles.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Key Section of Bharatmala Pariyojana, Boosting Regional Connectivity
articles.explainer:
• A vital section of the Bharatmala Pariyojana, a flagship highway development program, was inaugurated by the Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways.
• The newly opened stretch significantly improves connectivity between major economic hubs and reduces travel time and logistics costs in the region.
• Bharatmala Pariyojana aims to optimize freight and passenger movement across the country through the development of economic corridors, inter-corridors, and feeder routes.
• The project emphasizes greenfield expressways and upgrading existing national highways to world-class standards.
• This infrastructure push is expected to stimulate regional economic activity, create employment, and enhance overall national integration.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.

articles.trivia: The Bharatmala Pariyojana envisages the construction of 83,677 km of roads at an investment of ₹6.92 lakh crore by 2022-23 (original target).

articles.headline: Indus-X Showcase Highlights India-US Defence Startups Collaboration and Innovation
articles.explainer:

• The event aimed to foster collaboration in cutting-edge defense technologies, promoting co-development and co-production between Indian and American firms.
• Startups presented solutions in areas like AI, autonomous systems, cybersecurity, and advanced materials, crucial for future military capabilities.
• Indus-X, launched under the iCET framework, seeks to integrate the defense industrial ecosystems of India and the US, strengthening strategic ties.
• This platform is vital for leveraging private sector innovation to meet the defense requirements of both nations and enhance interoperability.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Standing Committee Reviews National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Progress
articles.explainer:
• A Parliamentary Standing Committee on Education conducted a review meeting on the implementation status of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The committee assessed progress in key areas such as curriculum reforms, teacher training, early childhood care and education (ECCE), and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges related to funding, infrastructure development, and faculty recruitment were highlighted, particularly in higher education.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased access to quality education at all levels.
• The review emphasized the need for accelerated implementation and greater coordination between central and state governments to achieve the policy's transformative goals.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Sherpa Meeting to Prepare Agenda for Next Leaders' Summit
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a G20 Sherpa meeting, bringing together representatives from member countries to finalize the agenda and draft declarations for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions centered on key global challenges including sustainable development, climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and inclusive economic growth.
• India, as a significant voice for the Global South, advocated for equitable solutions and emphasized the need for collective action on trans-national issues.
• The Sherpas worked towards building consensus on various policy proposals to be presented to the heads of state and government.
• This preparatory meeting is crucial for shaping the outcomes of the G20 Summit and reinforcing India's leadership in global governance.

articles.headline: India's Largest Floating Solar Energy Park Inaugurated in Southern State
articles.explainer:
• The Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy inaugurated India's largest floating solar energy park, located in a major reservoir in a southern Indian state.
• The park, with a capacity of 200 MW, will significantly contribute to the state's renewable energy targets and reduce carbon emissions.
• Floating solar projects conserve land, reduce water evaporation from reservoirs, and benefit from the cooling effect of water, enhancing efficiency.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
• The project showcases technological innovation in renewable energy and provides a model for sustainable power generation in water-rich regions.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

articles.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Impact Report Highlights Growth in Tier 2 and 3 Cities
articles.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released an impact report on the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The report highlighted significant growth in startup ecosystems in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, indicating broader entrepreneurial penetration.
• SISFS provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• The scheme has successfully supported ventures across diverse sectors, including agri-tech, healthcare, and clean energy.
• The positive impact underscores the government's commitment to fostering a vibrant startup culture and decentralized economic development.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Ethical AI Governance and Inclusive Access at Global AI Summit
articles.explainer:
• India participated in a Global AI Summit, advocating for a human-centric approach to Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance and promoting inclusive access to AI technologies.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for international cooperation to develop ethical guidelines, address biases, and ensure responsible deployment of AI.
• Discussions included leveraging AI for sustainable development goals, particularly in healthcare, agriculture, and education.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts, such as the National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence, focusing on 'AI for All' and building indigenous AI capabilities.

articles.headline: New Coastal Erosion Mitigation Project Launched in Vulnerable States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Earth Sciences, in collaboration with state governments, launched a new project to address coastal erosion in vulnerable regions across India.
• The initiative involves deploying nature-based solutions like mangrove restoration, coral reef protection, and artificial reefs, alongside engineered structures.
• The project aims to protect coastal communities, infrastructure, and biodiversity from rising sea levels and extreme weather events.
• Capacity building for local communities and integration of early warning systems are key components of the strategy.
• This comprehensive approach underscores India's commitment to coastal zone management and climate change adaptation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.

articles.headline: India's Q2 FY25 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Signals Economic Resilience
articles.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the second quarter of the financial year 2024-25 (July-September) surpassed market expectations.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in manufacturing, construction, and services sectors, alongside resilient domestic demand.
• This positive indicator reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, despite prevailing international economic headwinds.
• Government spending on infrastructure and continued private consumption contributed significantly to the upward trend.
• The encouraging figures provide impetus for further economic reforms and investment, boosting investor confidence.

highlights: Government unveils New Industrial Policy 2024, targeting 25% manufacturing share in GDP by 2030, with enhanced PLI schemes.
Supreme Court constitutes special bench to review petitions seeking clarification on its Electoral Bonds verdict, crucial for political funding transparency.
ISRO successfully conducts advanced test of its 'Pushpak' Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator, aiming to reduce space launch costs.
RBI Monetary Policy Committee holds repo rate steady at 6.5% for fifth consecutive meeting, maintaining 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance.
PM Jan Dhan Yojana achieves a new milestone, opening over 50 crore bank accounts, significantly boosting financial inclusion across India.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the New Industrial Policy 2024, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to achieve a 25% share of manufacturing in GDP by 2030.
2. It explicitly excludes the expansion of Production Linked Incentive (PLI) schemes.
3. It focuses solely on attracting foreign investment, neglecting domestic industries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The New Industrial Policy 2024 aims to achieve a 25% share of manufacturing in GDP by 2030. Statement 2 is incorrect; the policy introduces enhanced sector-specific incentives under the PLI scheme. Statement 3 is incorrect; it aims to attract both domestic and foreign investment and emphasizes MSME growth.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following statements about the 'Pushpak' Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator recently tested by ISRO is/are correct?
1. It is designed to significantly increase the cost of space launches.
2. The test validated technologies for autonomous landing and thermal protection systems.
3. India is the first country globally to successfully demonstrate RLV technology.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect; RLV technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches. Statement 2 is correct; the test validated critical technologies for autonomous landing, precise navigation, and thermal protection systems. Statement 3 is incorrect; while a significant achievement, India is among a select few nations pursuing advanced reusable rocket technology, not the first.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decisions:
1. The MPC's primary objective is to maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
2. The repo rate is the rate at which the RBI borrows money from commercial banks.
3. A 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance implies increasing liquidity in the banking system.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as per the RBI Act. Statement 2 is incorrect; the repo rate is the rate at which the RBI lends money to commercial banks, while the reverse repo rate is when RBI borrows from banks. Statement 3 is incorrect; 'withdrawal of accommodation' implies a focus on reducing liquidity in the banking system.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following is/are key objective(s) of the updated National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC)?
1. Introduce more ambitious targets for renewable energy deployment.
2. Integrate climate resilience strategies across various sectors.
3. Promote green financing and strengthen international cooperation for climate action.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The updated NAPCC introduces more ambitious targets for renewable energy, integrates climate resilience across sectors like agriculture and water, and emphasizes green financing and international cooperation.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY), consider the following:
1. It is a scheme for universal access to banking facilities with at least one basic banking account for every household.
2. It provides an overdraft facility of ₹10,000 for eligible account holders.
3. Women account holders constitute a majority of the new accounts opened under the scheme.
How many of the above statements are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. PMJDY aims for universal access to banking, provides an overdraft facility of ₹10,000 to eligible account holders, and has seen a majority of new accounts opened by women, signifying its impact on gender financial empowerment.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the salient features of the New Industrial Policy 2024. How far is it expected to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities and integrate it into global value chains?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment. Industrial policy changes and their effects on industrial growth.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the New Industrial Policy 2024 and its context. Discuss key features like technology adoption, skill development, ease of doing business, green industries, and PLI expansion. Analyze how these features aim to boost manufacturing (e.g., increased investment, competitiveness, MSME growth). Evaluate its potential for global value chain integration, considering challenges like infrastructure, logistics, and R&D. Conclude with a balanced perspective on its transformative potential.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the significance of the Supreme Court's verdict on Electoral Bonds for democratic transparency in India. What challenges persist in ensuring complete transparency in political funding, and what legislative reforms could be considered?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions. Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by explaining the Supreme Court's original verdict on Electoral Bonds and its rationale (Right to Information). Elaborate on why transparency in political funding is crucial for democracy. Discuss challenges like anonymous donations, corporate influence, and enforcement. Suggest legislative reforms such as cap on corporate donations, state funding of elections, and independent audit mechanisms. Conclude with the importance of judicial activism and legislative action for a robust democracy.

mainsMocks.question: India has been a consistent and significant contributor to UN Peacekeeping operations. In this context, critically examine India's call for comprehensive reforms in UN Peacekeeping, particularly concerning mandate clarity and inclusivity. How does this align with India's broader foreign policy objectives?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Briefly introduce India's role as a major troop-contributing country to UN Peacekeeping. Detail India's specific reform demands: mandate clarity (realistic goals, clear exit strategies), adequate resources, safety of peacekeepers, and greater inclusivity (especially women peacekeepers). Link these demands to the effectiveness and legitimacy of UN missions. Connect this advocacy to India's foreign policy goals of promoting multilateralism, global peace, humanitarian values, and its aspiration for a permanent seat in the UNSC. Conclude on the need for collective global action and reforms.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Review Petitions Seeking Clarification on Electoral Bonds Verdict
GS2.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has been constituted to hear petitions seeking clarification and implementation guidelines following its earlier verdict on electoral bonds.
• The petitioners are seeking clarity on the disclosure of donor identities and the mechanism for ensuring transparency in political funding.
• The original verdict in February 2024 had struck down the Electoral Bonds Scheme as unconstitutional, citing violation of the right to information.
• This review is crucial for defining the future of political funding transparency in India and ensuring compliance by political parties and the Election Commission.
• The court's observations are expected to provide a roadmap for legislative reforms in campaign finance.

Election Commission of India plays a role in implementing electoral reforms.

Discuss the challenges in ensuring complete transparency in political funding in India and suggest potential reforms.
Evaluate the arguments for and against the anonymity of political donations in a democratic setup.
Examine the role of the judiciary in upholding constitutional principles related to electoral processes.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Hold Joint Economic Commission Meeting, Focus on Trade Expansion and Investment
GS2.explainer:
• The 9th India-Brazil Joint Economic Commission (JEC) meeting concluded, emphasizing strengthening bilateral trade and investment ties.
• Discussions focused on diversifying trade basket, reducing non-tariff barriers, and exploring cooperation in agriculture, energy, and defense sectors.
• Both nations reiterated commitment to multilateralism and cooperation within BRICS, G20, and IBSA forums.
• Agreements were signed to promote greater ease of doing business and enhance collaboration in pharmaceuticals and digital technologies.
• The meeting aimed to leverage the strategic partnership to achieve a target of $15 billion in bilateral trade by 2026.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Hold Annual Defence Dialogue, Deepen Strategic Partnership in Indo-Pacific
GS2.explainer:

• The dialogue underscored the growing convergence of strategic interests between India and Japan, particularly in countering common security challenges.
• Future collaborations include joint research in emerging defense technologies and expanded information sharing mechanisms.

GS2.explainer:

• The Indian representative called for comprehensive reforms in UN peacekeeping, including mandate clarity, adequate resources, and enhanced safety for peacekeepers.
• Emphasis was placed on greater inclusivity, particularly the participation of women peacekeepers, to address complex challenges effectively.
• India stressed the importance of technology-driven solutions and capacity building for host countries to ensure sustainable peace.
• This stance aligns with India's broader foreign policy objectives of promoting multilateralism and global peace and security.

GS2.headline: PM Jan Dhan Yojana Achieves New Milestone, Over 50 Crore Bank Accounts Opened
GS2.explainer:
• The Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana (PMJDY), India's flagship financial inclusion program, announced the opening of over 50 crore (500 million) bank accounts.
• This milestone signifies significant progress in bringing unbanked populations into the formal financial system, particularly in rural and remote areas.
• The scheme provides access to basic savings accounts, debit cards, direct benefit transfer (DBT) facilities, and micro-insurance/pension products.
• Women account holders constitute a majority of the new accounts, highlighting the scheme's impact on gender financial empowerment.
• PMJDY has been instrumental in enabling direct transfer of government welfare benefits, reducing leakages and improving efficiency.

GS2.explainer:

• The committee assessed progress in key areas such as curriculum reforms, teacher training, early childhood care and education (ECCE), and digital learning initiatives.
• Challenges related to funding, infrastructure development, and faculty recruitment were highlighted, particularly in higher education.
• The NEP 2020 aims for holistic and multidisciplinary education, skill development, and increased access to quality education at all levels.
• The review emphasized the need for accelerated implementation and greater coordination between central and state governments to achieve the policy's transformative goals.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Sherpa Meeting to Prepare Agenda for Next Leaders' Summit
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a G20 Sherpa meeting, bringing together representatives from member countries to finalize the agenda and draft declarations for the upcoming Leaders' Summit.
• Discussions centered on key global challenges including sustainable development, climate finance, digital public infrastructure, and inclusive economic growth.
• India, as a significant voice for the Global South, advocated for equitable solutions and emphasized the need for collective action on trans-national issues.
• The Sherpas worked towards building consensus on various policy proposals to be presented to the heads of state and government.
• This preparatory meeting is crucial for shaping the outcomes of the G20 Summit and reinforcing India's leadership in global governance.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Industrial Policy 2024 to Boost Manufacturing and Global Competitiveness
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved the new Industrial Policy 2024, focusing on enhancing India's manufacturing capabilities and integrating into global value chains.
• Key pillars include technology adoption, skill development, ease of doing business, and promotion of green industries.
• The policy aims to attract significant domestic and foreign investment, targeting a 25% share of manufacturing in GDP by 2030.
• It introduces sector-specific incentives under the Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme and streamlines regulatory approvals.
• Special emphasis is placed on MSME growth, innovation ecosystems, and sustainable industrial practices.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Conducts Advanced Test of Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) achieved a significant milestone with the successful landing experiment of its Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology Demonstrator.
• This test validated critical technologies for autonomous landing, precise navigation, and thermal protection systems crucial for future reusable spacecraft.
• RLV technology aims to drastically reduce the cost of space launches, making space access more affordable and sustainable.
• The demonstrator, named 'Pushpak,' performed a precise landing at a designated airstrip, simulating return from space.
• This development places India among a select few nations pursuing advanced reusable rocket technology, bolstering its position in the global space market.

GS3.headline: Government Releases Updated National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) with Enhanced Targets
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change released an updated National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC), aligning with India's enhanced Nationally Determined Contributions (NDCs).
• The revised plan introduces more ambitious targets for renewable energy deployment, energy efficiency, and afforestation efforts.
• It integrates climate resilience strategies across various sectors, including agriculture, water resources, and urban development.
• Emphasis is placed on leveraging technology and innovation, promoting green financing, and strengthening international cooperation for climate action.
• The update reflects India's commitment to achieving its climate goals while ensuring sustainable development and inclusive growth.

GS3.headline: RBI Monetary Policy Committee Holds Repo Rate Steady Amidst Global Economic Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the fifth consecutive meeting.
• The decision reflects a cautious approach amidst persistent global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and domestic inflationary pressures.
• RBI Governor highlighted the need to remain vigilant on inflation, despite a recent moderation, and reaffirmed commitment to achieving the 4% inflation target.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a focus on reducing liquidity in the banking system.
• Experts suggest the pause aims to support economic growth while ensuring price stability, with future rate cuts dependent on sustained disinflation.

GS3.headline: Government Reviews National Logistics Policy Implementation, Focus on Reducing Costs and Improving Efficiency
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry conducted a comprehensive review of the National Logistics Policy (NLP) implementation, two years after its launch.
• The review highlighted progress in creating unified logistics interface platforms (ULIP) and improving multimodal connectivity.
• Discussions focused on addressing bottlenecks, leveraging technology for better supply chain management, and reducing logistics costs from 14% to 8% of GDP.
• Initiatives like the PM Gati Shakti National Master Plan are crucial for achieving the NLP's objectives of integrated infrastructure development.
• The government aims to make India a global logistics hub by 2030, enhancing export competitiveness and domestic trade efficiency.

GS3.trivia: India ranks 38th in the Logistics Performance Index (LPI) 2023, a significant improvement from previous years.

GS3.headline: Indigenous Dengue Vaccine Shows Promising Results in Phase 3 Clinical Trials
GS3.explainer:
• An indigenously developed dengue vaccine candidate has completed Phase 3 clinical trials, demonstrating high efficacy and safety profiles.
• The vaccine, developed by a leading Indian pharmaceutical company in collaboration with ICMR, targets all four serotypes of the dengue virus.
• This breakthrough is significant for India, which bears a substantial burden of dengue cases annually, and could lead to widespread public health benefits.
• Regulatory approval is expected in the coming months, paving the way for its introduction into the national immunization program.
• The development underscores India's growing capabilities in vaccine research and manufacturing, strengthening its 'Vaccine Maitri' initiative.

Examine the challenges and opportunities in integrating new vaccines into national immunization programs in a diverse country like India.
Evaluate India's growing capabilities as a global hub for vaccine research and manufacturing.

GS3.trivia: Dengue fever is caused by a virus transmitted by Aedes aegypti mosquitoes, which also transmit Zika, chikungunya, and yellow fever viruses.

GS3.headline: Government Finalizes National Cyber Security Strategy 2024 to Safeguard Digital Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) has finalized the National Cyber Security Strategy 2024, aiming to create a resilient and secure cyberspace for India.
• The strategy emphasizes a multi-stakeholder approach, involving government, private sector, academia, and civil society in cyber defense.
• Key focus areas include critical information infrastructure protection, capacity building, R&D in cybersecurity, and international cooperation.
• It proposes a framework for proactive threat intelligence sharing, incident response, and robust regulatory mechanisms.
• The strategy is crucial for protecting India's growing digital economy and ensuring national security in the face of evolving cyber threats.

Examine the role of international cooperation in building a secure global cyberspace and India's contributions.
GS3.imageDescription: Digital lock icon with lines of code in the background, symbolizing cybersecurity.
GS3.trivia: CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to computer security incidents in India.

GS3.headline: Union Minister Inaugurates Key Section of Bharatmala Pariyojana, Boosting Regional Connectivity
GS3.explainer:
• A vital section of the Bharatmala Pariyojana, a flagship highway development program, was inaugurated by the Union Minister for Road Transport and Highways.
• The newly opened stretch significantly improves connectivity between major economic hubs and reduces travel time and logistics costs in the region.
• Bharatmala Pariyojana aims to optimize freight and passenger movement across the country through the development of economic corridors, inter-corridors, and feeder routes.
• The project emphasizes greenfield expressways and upgrading existing national highways to world-class standards.
• This infrastructure push is expected to stimulate regional economic activity, create employment, and enhance overall national integration.

GS3.trivia: The Bharatmala Pariyojana envisages the construction of 83,677 km of roads at an investment of ₹6.92 lakh crore by 2022-23 (original target).

GS3.headline: Indus-X Showcase Highlights India-US Defence Startups Collaboration and Innovation
GS3.explainer:
• The Indus-X (India-U.S. Defense Acceleration Ecosystem) initiative hosted a major showcase event, featuring innovative defense startups from both countries.
• The event aimed to foster collaboration in cutting-edge defense technologies, promoting co-development and co-production between Indian and American firms.
• Startups presented solutions in areas like AI, autonomous systems, cybersecurity, and advanced materials, crucial for future military capabilities.
• Indus-X, launched under the iCET framework, seeks to integrate the defense industrial ecosystems of India and the US, strengthening strategic ties.
• This platform is vital for leveraging private sector innovation to meet the defense requirements of both nations and enhance interoperability.

GS3.trivia: The iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies) was launched in January 2023 to deepen strategic collaboration between India and the US.

GS3.headline: India's Largest Floating Solar Energy Park Inaugurated in Southern State
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Minister for New and Renewable Energy inaugurated India's largest floating solar energy park, located in a major reservoir in a southern Indian state.
• The park, with a capacity of 200 MW, will significantly contribute to the state's renewable energy targets and reduce carbon emissions.
• Floating solar projects conserve land, reduce water evaporation from reservoirs, and benefit from the cooling effect of water, enhancing efficiency.
• This initiative is part of India's broader strategy to achieve 500 GW of non-fossil fuel electricity capacity by 2030.
• The project showcases technological innovation in renewable energy and provides a model for sustainable power generation in water-rich regions.

GS3.trivia: The world's largest floating solar power plant (600 MW) is being constructed on the Omkareshwar Dam in Madhya Pradesh.

GS3.headline: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme Impact Report Highlights Growth in Tier 2 and 3 Cities
GS3.explainer:
• The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) released an impact report on the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS).
• The report highlighted significant growth in startup ecosystems in Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities, indicating broader entrepreneurial penetration.
• SISFS provides financial assistance to startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, and market-entry.
• The scheme has successfully supported ventures across diverse sectors, including agri-tech, healthcare, and clean energy.
• The positive impact underscores the government's commitment to fostering a vibrant startup culture and decentralized economic development.

GS3.headline: India Advocates for Ethical AI Governance and Inclusive Access at Global AI Summit
GS3.explainer:
• India participated in a Global AI Summit, advocating for a human-centric approach to Artificial Intelligence (AI) governance and promoting inclusive access to AI technologies.
• The Indian delegation emphasized the need for international cooperation to develop ethical guidelines, address biases, and ensure responsible deployment of AI.
• Discussions included leveraging AI for sustainable development goals, particularly in healthcare, agriculture, and education.
• India highlighted its domestic efforts, such as the National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence, focusing on 'AI for All' and building indigenous AI capabilities.
• The summit aimed to forge global consensus on regulating AI, mitigating risks, and harnessing its potential for societal benefit.

GS3.headline: New Coastal Erosion Mitigation Project Launched in Vulnerable States
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Earth Sciences, in collaboration with state governments, launched a new project to address coastal erosion in vulnerable regions across India.
• The initiative involves deploying nature-based solutions like mangrove restoration, coral reef protection, and artificial reefs, alongside engineered structures.
• The project aims to protect coastal communities, infrastructure, and biodiversity from rising sea levels and extreme weather events.
• Capacity building for local communities and integration of early warning systems are key components of the strategy.
• This comprehensive approach underscores India's commitment to coastal zone management and climate change adaptation.

GS3.headline: India's Q2 FY25 GDP Growth Exceeds Projections, Signals Economic Resilience
GS3.explainer:
• India's Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth for the second quarter of the financial year 2024-25 (July-September) surpassed market expectations.
• The robust growth was primarily driven by strong performance in manufacturing, construction, and services sectors, alongside resilient domestic demand.
• This positive indicator reinforces India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, despite prevailing international economic headwinds.
• Government spending on infrastructure and continued private consumption contributed significantly to the upward trend.
• The encouraging figures provide impetus for further economic reforms and investment, boosting investor confidence.
